desc: Fethiye is one of the most flexible bases on Turkey's Turquoise Coast: a working harbour town with Lycian tombs above it, easy access to Ölüdeniz and Babadağ, boat days into steep-sided coves, and enough everyday life that it does not feel like a sealed resort enclave. It works best for travellers who want to mix beaches, short drives and active days rather than stay planted in one all-inclusive strip.

todo.name: Lycian rock tombs
todo.desc: Climb above town to see the carved Amyntas tombs and wide views back over Fethiye bay.

todo.name: Oludeniz and Blue Lagoon
todo.desc: Base a beach day around the lagoon, then watch paragliders descend from Babadag.

todo.name: Butterfly Valley boat trip
todo.desc: Use a boat day to reach steep-sided coves that are awkward by road.

todo.name: Saklikent Gorge
todo.desc: Cool off inland with a gorge walk and river-platform lunch during hotter months.

todo.name: Paspatur old town and fish market
todo.desc: Use the older centre for a more grounded evening than the seafront alone, especially if you want shops, meze and people-watching.

todo.name: Kayakoy ghost village
todo.desc: Head uphill to the abandoned Greek village for a more haunting counterpoint to the coast-and-boat side of the area.

wifi.note: Usually solid in town hotels, marina cafes and larger apartment stays, but boat trips, gorge outings and smaller pension-style places are less reliable than the centre.

prac.fltNote: Fethiye has no airport and no destination-level LGW/LCY nonstop routing. Dalaman is the practical gateway and does have London Gatwick service at airport level, with easyJet currently selling London Gatwick-Dalaman nonstop flights, but the final transfer to Fethiye or Ölüdeniz still matters. Summer school holidays tighten prices fast, while afternoon road traffic toward Ölüdeniz can add another layer of friction after landing.
prac.lang: Turkish; English varies outside resorts and major hotels
prac.visa: British citizens can visit Turkey visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period, and GOV.UK says your passport must be valid for at least 150 days after the date you arrive with at least 1 blank page.
bestFor: Late spring and early autumn sun
alerts: Oludeniz road traffic is slow in peak summer afternoons.
Boat days can be wind-affected outside the settled summer pattern.
Saklikent water levels and gorge access vary by season.
July and August heat can make tomb climbs, market walks and non-beach sightseeing feel punishing by midday.

sweet: May, June and September are usually the best balance for warm sea weather, full boat and paragliding operations, and less pressure than the hottest midsummer weeks.
